title: InstCombine: Fix a crash in `PointerReplacer` when constructing a new PHI
summary:
The developers generalized an optimization by recognizing that a specific pattern—where a `LoadInst` (`LT`) is replaced by a new instruction (`NewI`)—needed proper tracking in the `WorkMap` to enable subsequent transformations. Initially, the map incorrectly stored the old instruction (`LT`) as its own replacement, causing missed optimizations. The fix maps `NewI` to itself, ensuring the replacement is correctly recognized as the new value for future passes.

This approach transforms a one-off fix into a reusable pattern: when replacing an instruction, update the work map with the new instruction as the canonical value, not the old one. This prevents stale entries and ensures consistency across iterative combines. Developers can apply this by always verifying that replacement mappings in transformation caches point to the current active value, especially when the original instruction is erased. This small change enhances the robustness of pattern-matching passes that depend on accurate value tracking.
